screenshot_note: Code-level review — focused on bugs, performance, and UX issues identified from source analysis
visual_observations: Office view is charming — SVG characters with vibe-reactive Bloberto face is genuinely delightful
Dashboard cards, activity log, stats bar all feel polished. Good use of color coding
Confetti + completion toast on task ship is a nice touch
The whiteboard and coffee corner add personality without clutter
Maya and Carlos currently working on Luna's ideas — live site should show them at desks

suggestions.title: ProgressBar milestone Set never resets when worker restarts a task
suggestions.description: ProgressBar uses milestonesRef = useRef(new Set()) to track which milestones (25/50/75/100) have flashed. This ref persists across task changes because it's never cleared. When a worker completes (progress=100) then starts a new task (progress resets to 0), the Set still has {25,50,75,100}, so no flash animations fire for the new task. Fix: add a useEffect that clears milestonesRef.current when progress drops below 25 or when updatedAt changes significantly (indicating new task). Small fix, big visual impact — milestones are a key feedback loop.

suggestions.title: WORKER_ROLE_COLORS duplicates and diverges from ROLE_COLORS in constants.js
suggestions.description: App.jsx lines 14-20 define WORKER_ROLE_COLORS keyed by worker NAME (Maya: '#a855f7'). constants.js defines ROLE_COLORS keyed by ROLE ('Frontend Engineer': '#a78bfa'). The hex values have silently diverged (Maya's purple is #a855f7 vs Frontend's #a78bfa). More critically, WORKER_ROLE_COLORS is keyed by name — renaming a worker silently breaks confetti colors. Fix: delete WORKER_ROLE_COLORS entirely and use `ROLE_COLORS[worker.role] || '#a855f7'` at line 697 where confetti color is computed. Single source of truth.

suggestions.title: Character divs have onClick + tabIndex but no role='button' or keyboard handler
suggestions.description: Office.jsx Character components with onClick have tabIndex={0} but no role attribute and no onKeyDown handler. Screen readers announce them as generic groups, not interactive elements. Pressing Enter/Space while focused does nothing. Fix: add role='button' and onKeyDown={(e) => { if (e.key === 'Enter' || e.key === ' ') { e.preventDefault(); onClick?.(worker) } }} to the Character div when onClick is provided. Affects all working and idle characters with click handlers.

suggestions.title: ActivityLog time-group filtering runs 80 iterations every 60s tick with no memoization
suggestions.description: ActivityLog (App.jsx ~line 112) computes 'groups' by running TIME_GROUP_ORDER.map → displayed.filter for each of 4 groups × 20 entries = 80 filter calls. This runs on every render including the 60s useTimeTick. Wrap the groups computation in useMemo keyed on [entries]. Also: the IIFE inside WorkerCard's task tag render (lines ~272-292) creates a closure every render — extract to a variable.

suggestions.title: completionToast disappears with no exit animation — jarring UX
suggestions.description: The completion toast (App.jsx ~line 805) has a badge-pop entry animation but vanishes abruptly after 3s via setTimeout → setCompletionToast(null). No fade-out transition. Fix: at 2700ms, add a CSS class with opacity:0 transition (300ms), then unmount at 3000ms. Or use a single keyframe with animation-fill-mode:forwards that includes entry + exit.

suggestions.title: Mobile: ~250px dead zone below office floor before footer
suggestions.description: On 375×812 mobile, .main has flex:1 which expands beyond the office floor's content height (aspect-ratio:4/3 at 375px = ~320px), creating a dark empty gap before the footer. Fix: @media (max-width: 600px) { .main { flex: none; } .office-wrap { padding-bottom: 0.5rem; } } — content-fit instead of flex stretch.

suggestions.title: Ghost OOO bubble escapes office-floor overflow boundary on leftmost desks
suggestions.description: Ghost character OOO bubbles use position:absolute with bottom:calc(100% + 8px). On characters at left:7% desks, the bubble clips outside the office-floor's overflow:hidden due to CSS transform stacking contexts. Fix: use overflow:clip (not hidden) on .office-floor which isn't affected by stacking context interactions, or render OOO bubbles as a portal.

suggestions.title: stuckWorkers useMemo uses worker.started but some workers only have updated_at
suggestions.description: StatsBar line ~370 filters stuck workers by checking `Date.now() - new Date(w.started).getTime() > 7_200_000`. But some workers have started:null and only updated_at. This silently NaN's the comparison and those workers are never detected as stuck even if they've been working forever with only updated_at set. Fix: use `w.started || w.updated_at` as the timestamp source.

suggestions.title: No keyboard shortcut hint or help modal
suggestions.description: The app has keyboard shortcuts (1=Office, 2=Dashboard, R=Refresh, Escape=blur) but there's no discoverable UI for them. A small '?' button or footer hint showing 'Press ? for shortcuts' would improve power-user experience. Low priority but adds polish.

suggestions.title: Favicon canvas regenerates on every activeWorkers change even if color hasn't changed
suggestions.description: The favicon useEffect runs on [activeWorkers, fetchError] which changes frequently (every 30s poll). It regenerates the canvas even when the computed color hasn't changed. Fix: compute color first, store in a ref, and only regenerate canvas when color actually changes.
